title: Representative Eval Sets, Deterministic Oracles, Baselines, and Versions · Program Brief: Deterministic Oracles
summary: The team tests five hand-picked happy prompts, changes them after failures, has no task distribution or deterministic checks, and compares only final prose. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the deterministic oracles increment in versioned representative agent evaluation corpus. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.
Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example agent engineering team handles case 8139 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects. A defensible solution applies Deterministic Oracles, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned representative agent evaluation corpus.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-eval-dataset-oracles-4
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-eval-dataset-oracles-4.
# Case 1655: a controlled support-operations rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def worked_agentpy_eval_dataset_oracles_4(eval_cases: int = 72, normal_cases: int = 30, boundary_cases: int = 18, adversarial_cases: int = 18, accessibility_cases: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorityworked4",
        "interaction1655",
        "tool4dekrow",
        "stateworked41655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if eval_cases < 40:
        return False, eval_cases, "eval-depth"
    if normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases != eval_cases:
        return False, normal_cases, "eval-reconciliation"
    if boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8:
        return False, adversarial_cases, "eval-risk-coverage"
    if accessibility_cases < 4:
        return False, accessibility_cases, "eval-accessibility"
    assert normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases == eval_cases
    return True, eval_cases, "representative-agent-evals"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Deterministic Oracles evidence · Theory Deterministic Oracles
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-deterministic-oracles-h5zav6" that uses case 8679 to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-deterministic-oracles-h5zav6
# Competency: agentpy-deterministic-oracles.
# Case 8679: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def evidence_agentpy_deterministic_oracles_h5zav6(eval_cases: int = 72, normal_cases: int = 30, boundary_cases: int = 18, adversarial_cases: int = 18, accessibility_cases: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_h5zav6 = "h5zav6-6vaz5h-8679"
    assert evidence_variant_h5zav6
    evidence_axes_h5zav6 = (
        "authorityh5zav6",
        "interaction8679",
        "tool6vaz5h",
        "stateh5zav68679",
        "evaluation8679h5zav6",
        "recovery6vaz5h8679",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_h5zav6)) == 6
    if eval_cases < 40:
        return False, eval_cases, "eval-depth"
    if normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases != eval_cases:
        return False, normal_cases, "eval-reconciliation"
    if boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8:
        return False, adversarial_cases, "eval-risk-coverage"
    if accessibility_cases < 4:
        return False, accessibility_cases, "eval-accessibility"
    assert normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases == eval_cases
    return True, eval_cases, "representative-agent-evals"

content.text: A theory agent engineering team handles case 2186 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
